title: EPA Seeks to Roll Back PFAS Drinking Water Rules, Keeping Millions Exposed to Toxic 'Forever Chemicals' in Tap Water
summary: Legal challenge to the Trump EPA's partial rescission of PFAS MCLs. Documents the Earthjustice/state AGs argument that EPA lacks authority under the Safe Drinking Water Act to rescind finalized standards.

title: Trump EPA Rolls Back Biden's PFAS Drinking Water Rules
summary: Detailed reporting on the rollback decision, which compounds lost protection, and the communities — including Cape Fear, NC — most exposed to the rescinded standards.

title: How EPA Suppressed Its Own Science on PFNA's Health Risks Before Rolling Back Protections
summary: ProPublica documented that EPA scientists had flagged PFNA as more toxic than previously classified — but that assessment was not reflected in the rollback decision. Internal communications showed pressure to soften findings.
notes: TODO: Verify exact URL and date when published.

title: Trump EPA's PFAS moves create tension with MAHA agenda
summary: Examines the internal contradiction between MAHA's anti-toxin stance (RFK Jr.) and EPA's rollback of PFAS drinking water protections for four compounds.

title: PFAS Federal Policy Tracker
summary: Academic tracker of federal PFAS policy changes since January 2025. Includes legal analysis of the anti-backsliding provision in the Safe Drinking Water Act that limits EPA's authority to rescind finalized MCLs.

title: Coverage: EPA's PFAS rollback and community impacts
summary: Investigative coverage of EPA's PFAS actions, including the Cape Fear River corridor in North Carolina — where Chemours/DuPont GenX contamination is most severe — and the populations losing protection from the rescinded MCLs.

title: Trump EPA Weakens PFAS Chemical Reporting Requirements
summary: Analysis of the proposed TSCA Section 8(a)(7) reporting exemptions, which would allow industry to skip reporting PFAS below 0.1% concentration — a threshold covering many products.

title: EPA Launches PFAS-OUT Initiative to Help Proactively Address PFAS in Drinking Water
summary: Official announcement of the PFAS-OUT initiative. Emphasizes pro-cleanup framing while not mentioning forthcoming MCL rollbacks announced two months later. Key exhibit in the 'rhetoric vs. action' record.

title: Administrator Zeldin Announces Major EPA Actions to Combat PFAS Contamination
summary: Zeldin announces EPA leadership on PFAS, framing it as aggressive cleanup action. Key contrast document: two months later, EPA rescinded MCLs for GenX, PFHxS, PFNA, and PFBS.

title: Trump EPA Updates PFAS Destruction and Disposal Guidance to Protect American Communities
summary: Updated guidance on safely destroying and disposing of PFAS-containing materials. Non-binding guidance only — not a regulation. Presented as protective action concurrent with rollback of binding MCLs.

title: EPA's PFAS Strategic Roadmap: EPA's Commitments to Action 2021–2024
summary: Biden EPA's comprehensive PFAS roadmap that set the baseline: MCL rulemaking, CERCLA designation, TSCA reporting, and research investments. The Trump II EPA inherited this framework and has selectively maintained, modified, or reversed its components.

title: Inhance Technologies v. EPA — 5th Circuit Vacates TSCA Section 5 Orders
summary: The Fifth Circuit ruled EPA exceeded TSCA Section 5 authority by issuing 'significant new use' orders against a 40-year-old fluorination process. Vacated the orders. Sets a precedent that limits EPA's fastest PFAS enforcement tool — SNURs — while directing use of the slower Section 6 rulemaking process.

title: How's My Waterway — EPA Drinking Water Data Tool
summary: EPA's interactive tool for looking up drinking water quality data by location, including UCMR5 PFAS monitoring results and violation records. Key resource for understanding which communities are most affected by PFAS contamination.
